Lincang.risk: State-affiliated operator under export licensing regime; zero direct western access.
Lincang.role: One of the world's highest-grade germanium deposits. Germanium was concentrated by hydrothermal fluids from the Lincang granite batholith into ancient swamp coal seams. Operated by Lincang Xinyuan (Shenzhen listed).
Lincang.inv: Highest-grade germanium deposit in the world. Sets the upstream cost floor for Chinese primary production — anyone producing outside China must contend with Lincang's grade advantage when competing on price.
risks: State-affiliated operator under export licensing regime; zero direct western access.
Chinese export controls limit westward flow
Single operator concentration risk

Wulantuga.risk: State ownership means output is policy-driven rather than price-responsive.
Wulantuga.role: Largest coal-hosted germanium deposit by reserve size. Only ~15-25% of theoretical germanium content is commercially recovered due to specialist extraction requirements. Operated by Shengli Coal Group (state-owned).
Wulantuga.inv: Largest known germanium deposit by reserve size but only ~15-25% of theoretical content is commercially recovered. Theoretical supply headroom exists but isn't being realized by current operators.
risks: State ownership means output is policy-driven rather than price-responsive.
Low recovery rate limits supply upside
No western investment access

Yimin.risk: Fragmented operator base, opaque production data.
Yimin.role: Germanium-bearing lignite basin in Inner Mongolia. Worked by various state-affiliated operators. Recovery rates are low and output is more geographically dispersed than Wulantuga.
Yimin.inv: Additional Chinese coal-hosted germanium resource. Worked by various smaller state-affiliated operators; low recovery rates, limited price responsiveness.
risks: Fragmented operator base, opaque production data.
Grade too low at current prices
State control limits western access

Huize.stat: 443 t Ge resources (Chihong) · zinc-lead ore host
Huize.risk: Output tied to zinc pricing, not germanium demand signals.
Huize.role: Zinc-lead deposit with significant germanium content. Primary source of germanium recovered through the Chihong zinc smelter operations — a separate extraction pathway from coal-hosted germanium.
Huize.inv: Primary source of germanium recovered through Yunnan Chihong's zinc smelter operations. The zinc-ore pathway complements the coal-hosted pathway as Chinese germanium's second extraction route.
risks: Output tied to zinc pricing, not germanium demand signals.
Ge output tied to zinc production decisions
Chinese listed - limited western access

Yiliang + SYGT.risk: Limited public disclosure on reserves and production.
Yiliang + SYGT.role: Additional Yunnan zinc-lead deposits with germanium content. Feeds into Chinese primary germanium supply via associated smelter operations.
Yiliang + SYGT.inv: Additional Yunnan zinc-lead resources with germanium content. Feeds into Chinese primary germanium supply via associated smelter operations.
risks: Limited public disclosure on reserves and production.
Regional concentration risk
Ownership fragmentation

Spetsugli.risk: Sanctions-exposed; no western route to market.
Spetsugli.role: Russia's primary germanium deposit. Worked by JSC Germanium. Output is effectively unavailable to western buyers due to sanctions imposed after Russia's invasion of Ukraine.
Spetsugli.inv: Russia's primary germanium deposit. Effectively unavailable to western buyers due to sanctions. Output likely flows to Chinese and non-western buyers, reinforcing Chinese supply dominance over western markets.
risks: Sanctions-exposed; no western route to market.
JSC Germanium is part of Rostec, a sanctioned state defense conglomerate
Sanctions could be reversed but political timeline is entirely unpredictable

Big Hill.risk: DRC operating risk; reprocessing ramp-up delays possible.
Big Hill.role: Historic copper-cobalt mining tailings near Lubumbashi with significant germanium content. Being reprocessed by STL/Gécamines joint venture under an offtake agreement with Umicore.
Big Hill.inv: The single most important western supply development. ~700 t of germanium-bearing tailings being reprocessed by STL/Gécamines under Umicore offtake. Critical feedstock for Umicore as recycled-only supply saturates.
risks: DRC operating risk; reprocessing ramp-up delays possible.
Hydrometallurgical plant is new — ramp-up timeline uncertain
Exclusive Umicore offtake means no price competition

Red Dog.stat: World-class zinc-lead deposit with Ge · declining
Red Dog.risk: Declining toward planned end-of-mine in 2031 (Aktigiruq extension possible but not yet permitted).
Red Dog.role: World-class zinc-lead deposit in northwest Alaska, operated by Teck Resources. Declining toward planned end-of-mine in 2031, though Teck is exploring Aktigiruq extension. Germanium content recovered but volumes not separately disclosed.
Red Dog.inv: North America's only significant western germanium-bearing zinc operation. Teck Resources operates and refines through Trail smelter; exposure to US Defense Production Act Title III investments possible.
risks: Declining toward planned end-of-mine in 2031 (Aktigiruq extension possible but not yet permitted).
Germanium output volumes not publicly disclosed
Teck-Anglo American merger may change operational priorities

Lincang Xinyuan.stat: ~40 t Ge/yr extracted from Lincang coal
Lincang Xinyuan.risk: Chinese export controls limit westward flow of output.
Lincang Xinyuan.role: Shenzhen-listed operator of Lincang germanium-bearing coal. One of few Chinese germanium operators with a public listing, giving indirect investor exposure to Chinese germanium supply.
Lincang Xinyuan.inv: Shenzhen-listed operator of the highest-grade Chinese germanium deposit. Provides indirect public investor exposure to Chinese germanium supply, though A-share access is limited for non-Chinese investors.
risks: Chinese export controls limit westward flow of output.
A-share listing limits western investors
Single-site concentration risk

Shengli Coal Group.risk: Opaque production data and no western investment vehicle.
Shengli Coal Group.role: State-owned Chinese coal mining group operating the Wulantuga deposit in Inner Mongolia. Primary recovered germanium flows into Chinese refining chain; details of specific offtake arrangements not publicly disclosed.
Shengli Coal Group.inv: State-owned operator of the largest reserve-size germanium deposit. Output is policy-driven rather than price-responsive; limits upside even during supply constraints.
risks: Opaque production data and no western investment vehicle.
Power generation is primary — Ge always secondary
Policy shifts directly impact output

Various State Operators.role: Aggregate representing various smaller Chinese state-affiliated coal operators working the Yimin basin and other smaller germanium-bearing coal deposits.
Various State Operators.inv: Aggregate of smaller Chinese state-affiliated coal operators. Represents fragmented supply with limited responsiveness to price signals.
risks: Fragmented, opaque operator base.
Opaque operations with no public reporting
Output highly variable

Yunnan Chihong.stat: ~66 t Ge/yr refined (largest single Chinese source)
Yunnan Chihong.risk: Chinese export controls limit direct western access to the output.
Yunnan Chihong.role: Shanghai-listed zinc-lead mining and smelting company. Operates the largest single source of primary germanium globally via the Huize zinc smelter. Integrated from zinc concentrate through refined germanium output.
Yunnan Chihong.inv: Largest single source of primary germanium globally. Shanghai-listed — primary A-share vehicle for Chinese germanium exposure. Integrated zinc-ore-to-refined-germanium pathway through the Huize smelter.
risks: Chinese export controls limit direct western access to the output.
Ge output tied to zinc production
Zinc price weakness reduces Ge output

JSC Germanium.risk: Sanctions-exposed; no western route to market.
JSC Germanium.role: Russia's integrated germanium operator, working the Spetsugli deposit and operating downstream refining. Output is effectively unavailable to western buyers due to post-2022 sanctions; may flow to Chinese and other non-western buyers.
JSC Germanium.inv: Russia's integrated germanium operator. Effectively unavailable to western buyers due to sanctions. Tracked for geopolitical signaling but not investable.
risks: Sanctions-exposed; no western route to market.
Part of Rostec defense conglomerate
Production continues but serves only Russia and potentially China

STL / Gécamines.stat: ~30 t Ge/yr target · reprocessing Big Hill tailings
STL / Gécamines.risk: DRC operating risk; JV structure and political sensitivity around Gécamines.
STL / Gécamines.role: Joint venture between La Société de Traitement du Terril de Lubumbashi (STL) and Congolese state mining company Gécamines. Reprocessing Big Hill copper-cobalt tailings for germanium and other byproducts. Umicore has offtake rights on germanium output.
STL / Gécamines.inv: Most important western supply development in the chain. Feeds directly into Umicore under offtake — the tailings flow is what enables Umicore's continued non-Chinese output at scale.
risks: DRC operating risk; JV structure and political sensitivity around Gécamines.
Hydrometallurgical plant is new — ramp-up timeline uncertain
Exclusive Umicore offtake means no market price competition

Teck Resources.loc: Vancouver HQ, Canada; Red Dog mine, Alaska USA; Trail smelter, British Columbia, Canada
Teck Resources.stat: Undisclosed Ge from Red Dog zinc · North America's only western Ge source
Teck Resources.risk: Red Dog declines toward 2031 end-of-mine; Ge output volumes not separately disclosed.
Teck Resources.role: Diversified Canadian mining company. Operates Red Dog zinc mine in Alaska — the only significant germanium-bearing zinc operation in North America. Trail smelter in British Columbia processes concentrate but germanium output volumes not separately disclosed in filings.
Teck Resources.inv: North America's only significant western germanium operator via Red Dog. Germanium is a small line in Teck's overall business but carries strategic weight for US supply chain diversification.
risks: Red Dog declines toward 2031 end-of-mine; Ge output volumes not separately disclosed.
Teck-Anglo American merger may change operational priorities at Red Dog
Germanium revenue is unquantified

Umicore.loc: Olen, Belgium (Ge facility); Brussels HQ, Belgium
Umicore.stat: ~40-50 t/yr recycled Ge · fiber-grade GeCl₄, IR optics, wafers
Umicore.risk: Single-facility risk at Olen; business distractions from other Umicore segments.
Umicore.role: Diversified Belgian materials technology group. Operates the only commercial-scale fiber-grade GeCl₄ facility outside China and Russia, in Olen, Belgium. Processes essentially all non-Chinese, non-Russian germanium supply including the DRC tailings feedstock from Umicore-STL/Gécamines offtake. Structural western chokepoint.
Umicore.inv: The western chokepoint for fiber-grade germanium tetrachloride. Operates the only commercial-scale GeCl₄ facility outside China and Russia. The December 2024 Chinese export ban made Umicore's position structural rather than discretionary.
risks: Single-facility risk at Olen; business distractions from other Umicore segments.
DRC Big Hill ramp timeline uncertain
Recycled scrap feed shrinks if Chinese fiber output declines

Yunnan Chihong Refinery.stat: ~66 t/yr refined Ge · GeO₂, Ge metal, GeCl₄
Yunnan Chihong Refinery.risk: Output primarily stays within Chinese domestic ecosystem.
Yunnan Chihong Refinery.role: Refining and chemical conversion arm of Yunnan Chihong. Produces fiber-grade GeCl₄ in addition to GeO₂ and metal forms. One of four fiber-grade GeCl₄ facilities in China and a major feeder of Chinese domestic fiber preform production.
Yunnan Chihong Refinery.inv: One of four fiber-grade GeCl₄ facilities in China. Integrated with upstream Chihong zinc-ore germanium extraction. Major feeder of Chinese domestic fiber preform production at YOFC, Hengtong, and ZTT.
risks: Output primarily stays within Chinese domestic ecosystem.
Single-facility concentration
Dependent on zinc smelter economics

Chinese State Refiners.risk: Opaque individual facility data; output subject to Chinese export controls.
Chinese State Refiners.role: Aggregate representing smaller Chinese germanium refiners with limited public disclosure. Focus on GeO₂ and crude metal output; limited high-purity GeCl₄ capacity at individual facilities.
Chinese State Refiners.inv: Aggregate of smaller Chinese refiners. Focus on GeO₂ and crude metal; limited fiber-grade GeCl₄ capacity at individual facilities.
risks: Opaque individual facility data; output subject to Chinese export controls.
High exit risk when prices fall
Opaque output reporting

JSC Germanium Refinery.stat: ~20 t/yr refined · Ge metal, GeO₂, GeCl₄, IR optics, wafers
JSC Germanium Refinery.role: Integrated Russian germanium refiner producing a full range of germanium products from metal to wafers. Sanctioned; output is unavailable to western buyers and likely flows to Chinese and non-western customers.
JSC Germanium Refinery.inv: Integrated Russian germanium refiner with full product range. Sanctioned; output unavailable to western buyers.
risks: Sanctions-exposed; not investable.
Serves Russian domestic and potentially Chinese markets only
Represents structural supply removal from western accessible market

5N Plus.loc: Montreal, Quebec (HQ); St. Louis, Missouri, USA (operations)
5N Plus.stat: ~15-20 t/yr est. · Ge wafers, GeO₂, high-purity metal
5N Plus.risk: Pre-commercial on fiber-grade GeCl₄; qualification timeline and customer adoption unclear.
5N Plus.role: Montreal-listed specialty metals company. Refines germanium, gallium, tellurium, indium, and bismuth. Pre-commercial on fiber-grade GeCl₄ but pursuing qualification; would become the second western high-purity germanium refiner if qualified.
5N Plus.inv: Western specialty refiner pursuing fiber-grade GeCl₄ qualification. Would become the second western high-purity germanium refiner if qualified — materially reducing single-point-of-failure risk in western supply.
risks: Pre-commercial on fiber-grade GeCl₄; qualification timeline and customer adoption unclear.
Small scale — cannot meaningfully replace Chinese primary supply
Dependent on imported germanium feed

PPM Pure Metals.stat: ~10-15 t/yr est. · zone-refined Ge, GeO₂, GeCl₄
PPM Pure Metals.risk: Private — not directly investable. Scale limited.
PPM Pure Metals.role: Privately-held German specialty materials refiner. Produces zone-refined germanium for IR optics and SiGe semiconductor markets. Smaller scale than Umicore; part of western refining diversification but not at fiber-grade GeCl₄ volumes.
PPM Pure Metals.inv: Privately-held German specialty refiner. Zone-refined germanium for IR optics and SiGe markets; not at fiber-grade GeCl₄ volumes. Part of western refining diversification but smaller scale than Umicore.
risks: Private — not directly investable. Scale limited.
Small scale, niche market focus
Dependent on scrap feed from European fiber and IR optics manufacturers

Blue Moon Metals.stat: ~5-10 t/yr Ge target · only past-producing US Ge-Ga mine
Blue Moon Metals.risk: Pre-production junior; permitting, financing, and ramp-up risk typical of early-stage mining projects.
Blue Moon Metals.role: Toronto-listed junior acquired the Apex mine in Utah — the only past-producing primary germanium and gallium mine in the United States. Ge grades 10-100x higher than typical deposits; 1Mt resource. Backed by Oaktree Capital, Hartree Partners, Teck Resources (8% equity), Wheaton Precious Metals, and Altius Minerals. Production target 2028 pending permitting and financing.
Blue Moon Metals.inv: Most important US-domiciled germanium supply development. First new primary Ge mine on US soil in decades. Success would materially reduce US dependency on Chinese primary supply.
risks: Pre-production junior; permitting, financing, and ramp-up risk typical of early-stage mining projects.
No modern NI 43-101 resource estimate yet
2028 production target is aspirational

Chinese Primary Supply.risk: Export licensing can be tightened or released unilaterally.
Chinese Primary Supply.role: Aggregate of Chinese primary germanium supply — Lincang Xinyuan Refinery, Yunnan Chihong Refinery, and smaller Chinese refiners combined. Represents ~83% of global primary supply. Output subject to MOFCOM export licensing since August 2023.
Chinese Primary Supply.inv: ~83% of global primary supply. Output subject to MOFCOM licensing since August 2023. The core structural fact of the germanium chain.
risks: Export licensing can be tightened or released unilaterally.
Chinese domestic demand consuming growing share of output
No western alternative at equivalent scale

Western Recycled Supply.risk: Feedstock-limited; cannot scale independently of recycled pool or DRC tailings depletion.
Western Recycled Supply.role: Aggregate of western refining capacity — Umicore (Belgium), 5N Plus (Canada), PPM Pure Metals (Germany) combined. Predominantly processes recycled fiber optic scrap and IR optics rework plus the DRC tailings feedstock. ~9% of global refined supply reaches western buyers at significantly higher unit prices than Chinese domestic.
Western Recycled Supply.inv: Non-Chinese supply reaching western buyers. Predominantly recycled, with the DRC tailings feedstock ramping to feed Umicore. ~9% of global refined supply.
risks: Feedstock-limited; cannot scale independently of recycled pool or DRC tailings depletion.
Still partially dependent on Chinese primary Ge for high-purity applications
Western recycling capacity remains well below Chinese primary output

Global Supply.role: Aggregate of all global refined germanium supply — ~120 t primary (83% Chinese) + ~90-110 t recycled (western + some Chinese) + ~20 t Russian (sanctioned, not reaching west). Blended realized value reflects the bifurcated pricing between Chinese domestic and western markets.
Global Supply.inv: Blended total of ~230 t/yr refined germanium from all sources. Price realization varies dramatically by region due to MOFCOM licensing regime.
risks: Bifurcated market persists structurally.
Supply permanently capped by byproduct dependency
Chinese export controls create ongoing western supply risk

LightPath Technologies.loc: Orlando, Florida, USA (HQ); manufacturing in New Hampshire, Florida, Latvia, China
LightPath Technologies.stat: BlackDiamond IR glass substitute · $40M camera supply deal through 2027
LightPath Technologies.risk: Substitution penetration is slow; BlackDiamond requires design-in cycles that take years per customer platform.
LightPath Technologies.role: Orlando-listed IR optics and imaging systems company. Holds exclusive license to BlackDiamond chalcogenide glass that substitutes for germanium in infrared optics applications — a direct hedge against germanium scarcity. $40M camera supply contract through 2027; F-35 combat aircraft program validation.
LightPath Technologies.inv: The substitution hedge on germanium scarcity. If germanium prices remain elevated or supply tightens further, BlackDiamond glass captures displaced IR optics demand. Downstream of the germanium chain but structurally important to the investment thesis.
risks: Substitution penetration is slow; BlackDiamond requires design-in cycles that take years per customer platform.
Pre-profit company competing against Umicore's GASIR®
Germanium price decline would reduce urgency for substitution

Germanium Metal.risk: Illiquidity amplifies price moves in both directions; no exchange pricing transparency.
Germanium Metal.role: Physical germanium metal traded OTC. No futures market, no ETF, no exchange pricing. Market characterized by bilateral transactions between producers and consumers. Price has risen from ~$1,500/kg to over $8,500/kg in the western market in two years.
Germanium Metal.inv: Direct commodity exposure for investors wanting to express a view on germanium without equity-specific business risk. Physical accumulation is the only route — no liquid derivatives exist.
risks: Illiquidity amplifies price moves in both directions; no exchange pricing transparency.
Storage and handling requirements non-trivial
No futures market for hedging
